title: Objects
description: Group related values under named keys with plain JavaScript objects.
objectives: Create an object with `{ key: value }` literals
Iterate over an object's keys or entries
recap: Objects group related values under named keys. Read with `obj.key`, write with `obj.key = value`, and iterate with `Object.keys(obj)` or `Object.entries(obj)`. Arrays are for ordered lists; objects are for labeled properties.
practicePrompts: Count how many times each word appears in an array of words.
Given a map of prices, return the sum of all values.

practiceExercises.title: Word count
practiceExercises.prompt: Write a function `wordCount(words)` that takes an array of strings and returns an object mapping each unique word to how many times it appears.
practiceExercises.goal: Use an object as a hash map and increment counts.
hints: Build up an object as you loop through the array.
For each word, if it's not yet a key, start it at 1; otherwise increment.
`const counts = {}; for (const w of words) counts[w] = (counts[w] || 0) + 1; return counts;`

practiceExercises.title: Sum prices
practiceExercises.prompt: Write a function `priceSum(prices)` that takes an object mapping item names to prices and returns the total of all prices.
practiceExercises.goal: Use Object.values to sum object values.
hints: `Object.values(obj)` gives you an array of all the values.
Sum an array with `.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0)`.

practiceExercises.title: Key exists
practiceExercises.prompt: Write a function `hasKey(obj, key)` that returns `true` if the given key exists on the object, and `false` otherwise.
practiceExercises.goal: Use the 'in' operator or Object.keys to check for key presence.
hints: The `in` operator checks whether a key exists, regardless of its value.
`"name" in person` is `true` if `name` is a property on `person`.
